---
title: GitHub Copilot
description: Copilot adapter outputs, config toggles, and sync behavior.
section: adapters
---

## GitHub Copilot

The Copilot adapter renders Syntra's canonical `ai/` files into the file layout expected by GitHub Copilot in VS Code.

## Generated files

When Copilot sync runs, Syntra writes:

- `.github/copilot-instructions.md`
- `.github/agents/*.md`

These files are derived from your `ai/instructions/` and `ai/agents/` source files.

## Configuration

Copilot behavior is controlled in `ai/config.yml`:

```yaml
adapters:
	copilot:
		enabled: true
		instructionsMode: concatenated
```

| Key | Effect |
|---|---|
| `enabled` | If `true`, Copilot output is included in default `syntra sync` |
| `instructionsMode` | Controls how instruction files are assembled (`concatenated` or `individual`) |

## Sync behavior

Use either of these commands:

```bash
syntra sync
syntra sync --copilot
```

- `syntra sync` updates all enabled adapters
- `syntra sync --copilot` updates only Copilot files

Because this project currently has `adapters.copilot.enabled: true`, default sync refreshes `.github/`.

<Callout variant="warning" title="Do not hand-edit .github output">
	Any direct edits to `.github/copilot-instructions.md` or `.github/agents/*.md` are overwritten on the next sync.
</Callout>
